data.title: Canadian Imperial Bank Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.91, revenue of C$6.62B
data.description:

data.title: Canadian Imperial Bank raises dividend by 7.8% to CAD 0.97
data.description:
• Canadian Imperial Bank (NYSE:CM) declares CAD 0.97/share quarterly dividend, 7.8% increase from prior dividend of CAD 0.90.
  • Forward yield 4.34%
  • Payable Jan. 28; for shareholders of record Dec. 27; ex-div Dec. 27.
  • See CM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Tapestry prices $1.5B senior notes offering
data.description:
• Tapestry (NYSE:TPR) has priced its offering of $750M aggregate principal amount of 5.100% senior unsecured notes due 2030 and $750M aggregate principal amount of 5.500% senior unsecured notes due 2035.
  • The offering is expected to close, subject to normal closing conditions, on Dec. 11, 2024.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering, together with cash on hand, to repay outstanding loans under its term loan credit agreement and to repay borrowings under its revolving credit facility, which were used to fund the share repurchases under the company’s accelerated share repurchase agreements, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Hon Hai Precision Industry reports sharply sequential fall in November revenue
data.description:
Hon Hai Precision Industry (OTCPK:HNHAF) has reported revenue of NT$672.6 billion for November 2024, representing a decrease of 16.43% MoM and an increase of 3.47% YoY.

Segment break-down:

Computing Products: Strong growth MoM due to strong demand from the launch of new major products.

Components and Other Products: Revenue remained flattish MoM with stable performance.

Cloud and Networking Products: AI continued to drive server demand, while there was a decline MoM due to products entering a transition period between old and new models.

Smart Consumer Electronics Products: Revenue in November declined MoM owing to a high base in the previous month brought on by the demand in new product pull-in ahead of China’s Double 11 shopping festival and Thanksgiving holidays.

Outlook:

The second half of the year is traditionally peak season for the ICT industry, so operations are expected to gradually gain momentum quarter by quarter. Fourth quarter operations are anticipated to show significant growth on both a quarterly and yearly basis.

data.title: Janux Therapeutics prices $350M securities offering
data.description:
• Janux Therapeutics (NASDAQ:JANX) has announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 5.32 million shares of its common stock at a price of $63.00 per share, and pre-funded warrants to purchase 238,095 shares at a price of $62.999 per pre-funded warrant.
  • The gross proceeds to Janux from the offering are expected to be approximately $350.0 million.
  • Underwriters' overall allotment is an additional 833,333 shares of its common stock.
  • All the securities to be sold in the offering are to be sold by Janux.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 6, 2024.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to advance clinical development of its internal product pipeline and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor, Nvidia in talks to make AI chips at Arizona plant - report
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) is in talks with Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) to produce its Blackwell AI chips at the Taiwanese company's new plant in Arizona, Reuters reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

TSM is already making preparations to begin manufacturing early next year, the report added.

Nvidia's Blackwell chips were introduced by in March and have so far been made at TSMC's facilities in Taiwan. Nvidia has seen high demand for the chips from customers involved in generative AI and accelerated computing.

The agreement, if it comes to fruition, would secure another customer for TSM's Arizona plant, which is slated to begin volume production in 2025, the report noted.

Apple (AAPL) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) are currently customers at the Arizona facility, according to the report.

TSM intends to make the front-end process of Nvidia's Blackwell chips in Arizona but the processors will still need to be shipped back to Taiwan for packaging. The Arizona plant does not have chip on wafer on substrate, or CoWoS, capacity which is required for the Blackwell chips, the report added.

Currently, all of TSM's CoWoS capacity is in Taiwan, as per the report.

Last month, the Biden administration finalized its Chips Act incentive agreement with TSM, noting that the deal would spur $65B of private investment to build three facilities in Arizona and create tens of thousands of jobs by the end of the decade.

data.title: Euro Area retail sales down 0.5% m/m in October and down by 0.3% in the EU
data.description:
• Retail Sales In the Euro Area decreased 0.50% M/M in October 2024 over the previous month, and by 0.3% in the EU, according to first estimates from Eurostat, the statistical office of the European Union.

    • In September 2024, retail trade volume grew by 0.5% in the euro area and by 0.4% in the EU.

    • Retail Sales In the Euro Area increased 1.90% Y/Y in October 2024 over the same month in the previous year, and by 2.1% in the EU.

    ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

    Currency: (EUR:USD)

  • Weekly Bund Yield And Euro Forecast, November 1, 2024: Most Likely Range For 10-Year Bund Yields In 2034? 2% To 3%
  • European markets mixed as markets await Fed and BOE decisions
  • European stocks move higher following global stock rally
  • Euro Area retail sales rise 0.5% m/m in September

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: Crypto stocks, AEO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower in premarket trading on Thursday after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell remarked at a New York Times event that the U.S. economy remains resilient and stronger than the Federal Reserve had expected in September, prompting a cautious approach toward potential rate cuts.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Five Below (FIVE) shares jumped as much as 15% after the company delivered strong FQ3 results, with a 15% sales increase driving a 61% rise in profit and prompting an upward revision to its FY24 guidance. For Q4, sales are expected to range between $1.35B and $1.38B, with earnings of $3.15-$3.33 per share, compared to estimates of $1.36B and $3.29, respectively. Holiday comparable sales are forecasted to decline 3%-5%, better than the anticipated 5.44% drop. For the full year, the company raised its sales outlook to $3.84B-$3.87B and adjusted EPS guidance to $4.78-$4.96, surpassing estimates of $3.80B and $4.60 per share. Full-year comparable sales are expected to fall 3%, outperforming the predicted 4.71% decline. Additionally, the company announced Winnie Park as its new CEO, effective December 16.
  • Crypto-linked stocks soared as Bitcoin breached the $100,000 milestone, continuing its meteoric rise following Donald Trump's victory in the U.S. presidential election. The cryptocurrency has surged ~45% since Election Day, further bolstered by the nomination of cryptocurrency advocate Paul Atkins as the prospective chair of the Securities and Exchange Commission. Notable stock movements included MicroStrategy (MSTR) +7.5%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) 5.1%, MARA Holdings (MARA) +8%, Bitdeer Technologies (BTDR) +9.3%, and Coinbase (COIN) +4%.

Biggest stock losers

  • American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) shares plunged about 12% following mixed FQ3 results, missing revenue expectations and cutting its full-year outlook. For FQ4, the company projects comparable sales to grow by just 1%, with operating income expected between $125M and $130M. For FY2024, AEO lowered its comparable sales forecast to +3% compared to the earlier forecast of +4%, falling short of the consensus estimate of a 4.05% increase and total revenue to grow by 1%, down from the previous expectation of a +2% to +3%.
  • Shares of Synopsys (SNPS) dropped over 8% despite exceeding FQ4 expectations, as its outlook disappointed investors. For Q1 FY25, the company forecasted revenue of $1.44B-$1.47B, significantly below the $1.65B consensus, and adjusted EPS of $2.77-$2.82, falling short of the estimated $3.55. Nonetheless, the company expressed confidence in achieving double-digit revenue growth in 2025 and anticipates closing the Ansys acquisition in the first half of the year.
  • SentinelOne (S) shares plummeted 16% despite the cybersecurity company raising its FY2025 guidance following mixed FQ3 results. The company now expects revenue of $818M, slightly up from its previous forecast of $815M, compared to the consensus of $815.9M. CFO Barbara Larson noted, 'For the first time, we delivered positive free cash flow on a trailing-twelve-month basis, a key milestone in our journey toward sustained profitability.' She added that the company is raising its revenue growth outlook to 32% for fiscal year 2025, driven by strong execution and business momentum.

data.title: United Microelectronics November revenue grows 6.71% Y/Y to NT$20.05B
data.description:
• United Microelectronics (NYSE:UMC) reported November revenue of NT$20.05B, a rise of 6.71% Y/Y.
  • Year-to-date revenue climbed 3.79% to NT$213.34B.
  • YTD, the stock price was 20.5% down, it gets a Seeking Alpha quant rating of Hold with the highest factor grades given to valuation.

data.title: Eagle Point Credit announces $100M debt offering via issuance of notes due 2030
data.description:
• Eagle Point Credit (NYSE:ECC) has priced an underwritten public offering of $100 million principal amount of its 7.75% notes due 2030, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $96.5 million.
  • Underwriters' overall allotment is an additional $15M aggregate principal amount of 2030 notes.
  • The notes will be issued in denominations of $25 and integral multiples of $25 in excess thereof. They will bear interest at a rate of 7.75% per year, payable quarterly, with the first interest payment occurring on March 31, 2025.
  • The closing date is December 10.
  • The company intends to list the 2030 New York Stock Exchange notes under the symbol "ECCU."
